title: Square roots
description: Practice finding the square root of a perfect square positive integer.
topic_path: Get ready for exponents, radicals, & irrational numbers

hints: $\sqrt{9}$ is the number that, when multiplied by itself, equals $9$.
If you can't think of that number, you can break down $9$ into its prime factorization and look for equal groups of numbers.

Let's draw a factor tree.



[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
So the prime factorization of $9$ is $3\times 3$.
We're looking for $\sqrt{9}$, so we want to split the prime factors into two identical groups.
We only have two prime factors, and we want to split them into two groups, so this is easy.

So $\sqrt{9}$ is $3$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]
$\sqrt{16}$ is the number that, when multiplied by itself, equals $16$.
If you can't think of that number, you can break down $16$ into its prime factorization and look for equal groups of numbers.

Let's draw a factor tree.



[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
So the prime factorization of $16$ is $2\times 2\times 2\times 2$.
We're looking for $\sqrt{16}$, so we want to split the prime factors into two identical groups.

Notice that we can rearrange the factors like so:

 $16 = 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 = \left(2\times 2\right) \times \left(2 \times 2\right)$
So $\left(2\times 2\right)^2 = 4^2 = 16$.

So $\sqrt{16}$ is $4$.

 $36 = 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 3 = \left(2\times 3\right) \times \left(2 \times 3\right)$

$\sqrt{144}$ is the number that, when multiplied by itself, equals $144$.
If you can't think of that number, you can break down $144$ into its prime factorization and look for equal groups of numbers.

[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]
[[☃ image 1]]

So the prime factorization of $144$ is $2\times 2\times 2\times 2\times 3\times 3$.
We're looking for $\sqrt{144}$, so we want to split the prime factors into two identical groups.

Notice that we can rearrange the factors like so:

 $144 = 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 2 \times 3 \times 3 = \left(2\times 2\times 3\right) \times \left(2 \times 2 \times 3\right)$
So $\left(2\times 2\times 3\right)^2 = 12^2 = 144$.

So $\sqrt{144}$ is $12$.
